# Port status matrix

Target: WRF 4.7.1 at `f52c197ed39d12e087d02c50f412d90d418f6186`.

Whole-model parity: **0% (Not started)**. A Rust executable consumes WRF initialization and matches an upstream integration trajectory.

| Area | State | Evidence | Next gate | Tracking |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Source pin and license | Complete | UPSTREAM.toml and scripts/fetch-wrf.sh pin and verify WRF v4.7.1. | Retarget only through an explicit issue and compatibility audit. | — |
| ESMF-derived time/calendar | Complete active surface | All 93 active Test1 cases and both Fortran interfaces match the golden output. | Add cases when later WRF callers expose untested behavior. | — |
| Registry and configuration | In progress | Typed dimspec, state, rconfig, and package parsing includes nested source expansion, physical source locations, signed package conditions, ordered scalar groups, reserved-slot-aware PARAM/P/dense indices, source-order activation and deduplication, six exact artifact goldens, and a direct executable WRF package oracle. | Typedefs, communication entries, generalized four-dimensional scalar metadata, and complete scalar-index artifact generation. | [#85](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/85) |
| Domain decomposition and halo exchange | In progress | Exact task_for_point.c decomposition, period.c parity, and deterministic local and four-rank MPI results. | Generated communication descriptors, multi-field aggregation, nesting, and broader process grids. | [#88](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/88) |
| ARW dynamical core | In progress | Dependency-closed dry, acoustic, and specified-boundary kernels have direct pinned-Fortran parity; WRF physical boundary-zone assignment covers periodic, specified, nested, partial-tile, inactive, and IEEE cases; the complete failure-atomic three-substep nonhydrostatic acoustic boundary stage matches 149 complete affected-field snapshots per case and all final fields across full periodic/specified/nested, partial, inactive, and IEEE-injected cases. | Compose halo exchange, polar filtering, and distributed patch ownership around the verified acoustic boundary stage. | [#86](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/86) |
| Physics drivers and schemes | In progress | Kessler warm-rain microphysics matches all 660 mutable oracle values; the Registry-selected ARW preparation-driver-finish trajectory matches 35,280 pinned-Fortran raw-bit stage and checkpoint values through a reordered species layout, including inactive exceptional sentinels; active non-finite evolution is compiler-dependent, separate tests cover canonical ordering, checkpoint-split execution is exact, and one/four-worker results are deterministic. | Bind the accepted time-split Kessler trajectory into the Registry-backed ARW model runner; NetCDF resume remains issue #84. | [#82](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/82) |
| I/O and NetCDF metadata | In progress | The typed classic-NetCDF restart schema is driven by borrowed Registry state metadata across scoped dimension forms, staggering, external ordering, field types, and time levels, with direct pinned-Fortran ordering plus byte-identical NetCDF-C and raw-bit differential parity. | Alarm metadata, Registry boundary/subgrid/4D scalar forms, NetCDF-4 policy, and resumed-trajectory parity. | [#84](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/84) |
| WRFDA, WRF-Chem, WRF-Hydro, and TL/adjoint | Not started | No accepted compatibility slice. | Create separate workstreams after the ARW baseline. | — |
| End-to-end idealized and regression suite | Not started | No Rust executable trajectory accepted yet. | em_b_wave, then em_squall2d_x differential runs. | [#8](https://github.com/mikeortman/wrf-rs/issues/8) |

This matrix describes accepted interfaces and evidence, not a percentage extrapolated from file counts.
Mutable work state and sequencing live in GitHub Issues and the WRF Rust Port Project.
